name: Shipping Candies & Cookies
recipeIngredient: sturdy cardboard box or empty coffee can
aluminum foil or plastic food wrap
recipeInstructions:
1. Bar, drop, or fruit cookies are best for mailing as they withstand shipping better than fragile cookies that may crumble.
2. Use a heavy cardboard box, cookie tin, or empty coffee can as the mailing container.
3. Line the container with aluminum foil or plastic food wrap.
4. Wrap 4 to 6 cookies of the same size in aluminum foil, plastic food wrap, or plastic bags and seal securely with freezer tape.
5. Repeat until the container is full.
6. Place the heaviest cookies at the bottom and surround them with wrapped cookies crumpled paper towels.
7. You can use bubble wrap to line the container and separate layers of cookies if desired.
8. Seal the container with freezer, plastic or masking tape.
9. Wrap the container in an outer layer of brown mailing paper or a cut grocery sack.
10. Write the mailing and return addresses on waterproof ink.
11. Label the package as "Perishable Food" to ensure careful handling.
WARNING: Do not use aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
